content.problemStatement:
James has a board (a two-dimensional plane) with NNN nails sticking out of it and a rubber band tangled up between these nails. The rubber band can be viewed as a closed curve made up of MMM line segments (a closed polyline with MMM vertices). None of the nails intersect the rubber band, but the rubber band may intersect itself.

James wants to move the rubber band away from the nails. Specifically, the rubber band is removed if there is a line which does not intersect the rubber band or any of the nails and splits the board into two half-planes such that one half-plane contains all nails and the other half-plane contains the whole rubber band.

James can deform, stretch and shrink the rubber band in any way he wants, as long as it does not intersect with any nail (no point of the rubber band coincides with any nail) at any time during performing the deformations. However, he is not allowed to pick up the rubber band from the board (all deformations must occur in the given 2D plane).

Given the positions of the nails and the shape of the rubber band, determine if James will be able to remove the rubber band.
constraints: The distance of any point on the rubber band from any nail is at least 10^-4
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the number of nails, followed by N lines, each containing two space-separated real numbers x and y denoting the coordinates of a nail. Then, an integer M, the number of vertices of the rubber band, followed by M lines, each containing two space-separated real numbers x and y denoting the coordinates of a vertex of the rubber band.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string 'YES' if James can remove the rubber band or 'NO' if he cannot.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Example case 1: The rubber band is already separated from the only nail at position (90,90). Example case 2: The input corresponds to the image below.

solution.approach: To determine if James can remove the rubber band, we need to check if the nails are inside the rubber band. We can use the concept of the winding number to check if a point is inside a polygon. If the winding number of a point with respect to the rubber band is 0, then the point is outside the rubber band. Otherwise, it is inside. We calculate the winding number for each nail. If all nails have a winding number of 0, then James can remove the rubber band.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (N * M + M * log M)) — where T is the number of test cases, N is the number of nails, and M is the number of vertices of the rubber band. The time complexity is dominated by the calculation of the winding number for each nail and the sorting of the vertices of the rubber band.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N + M) — where N is the number of nails and M is the number of vertices of the rubber band. The space complexity is dominated by the storage of the nails and the vertices of the rubber band.
